sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 6, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: SPACE caused the white block to merge with the target frame, creating a larger white area (rows 36-40, cols 32-35). The blue center remains inside. The green block (player body) is at rows 41-43. This appears to be a "collection" mechanic - SPACE collects/activates nearby targets.
- Goal model: Collect all three blue-square targets by moving to each and pressing SPACE.
- Action model: Movement moves the player 4 cells. SPACE collects nearby targets.
- Plan: Move to the next target. Need to figure out player movement - try DOWN first to separate from collected target, then navigate to remaining targets.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 5 of about 71 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what happened after the DOWN action. I need to see if the player moved and what the current state looks like.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after DOWN
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}...")

# Check player position (green block)
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
white = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"\nGreen: {green[0]['boundary'] if green else 'NOT FOUND'}")
print(f"White: {white[0]['boundary'] if white else 'NOT FOUND'}")

# Check remaining targets (dark gray frames with blue centers)
c_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'c']
b_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']
print(f"\nCharcoal (c) nodes: {len(c_nodes)}")
for cn in c_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={cn['boundary']}, pixels={cn['pixels']}")
print(f"Blue (b) nodes: {len(b_nodes)}")
for bn in b_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={bn['boundary']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so after DOWN:
- Green block moved from rows 41-43 to rows 45-47 (moved down 4 rows)
- White block moved from rows 36-40 to rows 40-44 (moved down 4 rows)
- The blue center that was at rows 37-38 is now at rows 41-42 - it moved WITH the white block!
- The white block now contains the blue center inside it (the blue center is at rows 41-42, cols 33-34, which is inside the white block at rows 40-44, cols 32-35)
So the SPACE action "collected" the target - the white block absorbed the frame AND the blue center. Now the player carries the collected blue item.
The remaining uncollected targets are:
1. Top-right: frame at rows 24-27, cols 44-47, blue center at rows 25-26, cols 45-46
2. Left: frame at rows 28-31, cols 16-19, blue center at rows 29-30, cols 17-18
Current player position: green at rows 45-47, white at rows 40-44, cols 32-35.
I need to navigate to the remaining two targets. Let me plan the path:
- Player center is around row 45, col 33
- Left target: rows 28-31, cols 16-19 → need to go UP and LEFT
- Top-right target: rows 24-27, cols 44-47 → need to go UP and RIGHT
The player moves 4 cells per action. Let me figure out the exact positions needed.
For the left target (frame at rows 28-31, cols 16-19):
- Current green: rows 45-47, cols 32-35
- Need to move UP to get to around rows 28-31
- Need to move LEFT to get to around cols 16-19
For the top-right target (frame at rows 24-27, cols 44-47):
- Need to move UP to get to around rows 24-27
- Need to move RIGHT to get to around cols 44-47
Let me think about the optimal order. The player is at cols 32-35. The left target is at cols 16-19 (16 cols left) and the right target is at cols 44-47 (12 cols right). Both require going UP significantly.
Since the player moves 4 cells per action:
- To left target: need ~16/4 = 4 LEFT moves and ~17/4 ≈ 4-5 UP moves
- To right target: need ~12/4 = 3 RIGHT moves and ~21/4 ≈ 5-6 UP moves
Let me try going to the left target first since it's closer horizontally. I'll need to move UP and LEFT.
Actually, wait - I should think about whether the player needs to be exactly aligned with the target to collect it. From the first collection, the player was at rows 40-43 (green) with white at rows 36-40, and the target was at rows 36-39. The white block was adjacent to the target frame.
Let me try moving to the left target. I need to go UP and LEFT. Let me batch some moves.
Current position: green rows 45-47, cols 32-35
Left target: rows 28-31, cols 16-19
To get the white block adjacent to the target frame:
- White needs to be at rows ~28-31, cols ~16-19
- Currently white is at rows 40-44, cols 32-35
Row difference: 40 - 28 = 12 rows up → 3 UP actions
Col difference: 32 - 16 = 16 cols left → 4 LEFT actions
Let me try: LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, LEFT, UP, UP, UP to get to the left target area.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: DOWN moved the player (green + white blocks) down 4 rows. The blue center from the collected target is now carried inside the white block. Two targets remain: left (rows 28-31, cols 16-19) and top-right (rows 24-27, cols 44-47).
Goal model: Collect all three blue-square targets by moving adjacent and pressing SPACE.
Action model: Movement moves player 4 cells. SPACE collects adjacent targets.
Plan: Navigate to left target first (UP 3, LEFT 4), then SPACE, then navigate to top-right target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Navigate to left target: need UP ~3 times and LEFT ~4 times
# Current: green rows 45-47, cols 32-35; white rows 40-44, cols 32-35
# Left target: rows 28-31, cols 16-19
# Let me try LEFT 4 times then UP 3 times
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
